note: Application-level slices for green-ai. Commands, queries, orchestrations. No DB schema, no API endpoints, no UI. Aggregate boundaries respected throughout.

precision_notes_applied_from_060_review.decision: Non-SMS channel content (BroadcastEmailContent, WebNotification) marked as domain_scope: sms_derived_extension in affected slices. Not promoted to shared messaging candidates without explicit separate architectural decision.

precision_notes_applied_from_060_review.about: IDeliveryStatusWriter — contractual integration, not shared aggregate ownership
precision_notes_applied_from_060_review.decision: SL-MSG-07 and SL-MSG-08 carry explicit cross_aggregate_contract + ownership_note. Messaging owns write-surface semantics. Other channels have contract access only — no shared ownership.

precision_notes_applied_from_060_review.about: Address canonical state vs adapter staging/cache separation
precision_notes_applied_from_060_review.decision: Address slices divided: canonical-state read slices (SL-ADDR-01, SL-ADDR-02) vs adapter-import slices (SL-ADDR-03, SL-ADDR-04). canonical_state_note and adapter_staging_note added per slice. Staging tables are adapter-internal only.

slices.name: ComposeBroadcast
business_rules_enforced: BR024 — AlwaysOwner role forces lookupConfig.sendToOwner=true
BR025 — DontSendEmail role forces channels.email=false
BR026 — CanSpecifyLookup gates sendToOwner/sendToAddress fields
BR027 — CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate gates private/business lookup flags
external_dependencies: CUST-E04 ProfileRoleAssignment (role gate — via EvaluateProfileGate SL-CUST-02)
outputs_effects: Broadcast created in DRAFT state (active=false, isLookedUp=false)
BroadcastSmsContent / BroadcastEmailContent created if channels specified
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Not idempotent by default. API callers should provide client-side idempotency key. Role gate checked once at compose time — also re-checked at activation.

slices.name: AddRecipientCriterion
business_rules_enforced: BR001 — rejected if Broadcast.active=true
BR002 — rejected if Broadcast.isLookedUp=true
external_dependencies: SUB-E01 StandardReceiver (existence check if standardReceiverId provided)
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Concurrent adds are safe. BR001/BR002 guard prevents adds after activation.

slices.name: ActivateBroadcast
business_rules_enforced: BR004 — idempotent if already active (early return, no effect)
BR005 — reject if zero resolvable recipients
BR006 — warn if >25000 Kvhx (large broadcast)
BR007 — scheduledSendAt set → ALWAYS route async/batch, never sync
BR008 — sync gate: ByAddress + fromApi=true + ≤5 items + no zip-only → sync path
R037 — lastMinuteLookup=true AND scheduledSendAt set → skip lookup now, schedule ScheduledLookupTrigger 5 min before send
external_dependencies: CUST-E04 ProfileRoleAssignment (re-check gate at activation — via SL-CUST-02)
outputs_effects: Routes to SL-MSG-04 (sync path) OR SL-MSG-05 (enqueue async path)
If R037: queues ScheduledLookupTrigger — no immediate lookup
slices.idempotency_concurrency: IDEMPOTENT (BR004). Concurrent activations are safe — second call returns early. Dispatch routing happens at most once per broadcast.

routing_decision.sync_path: BR008=true AND BR007=false AND R037_skip_lookup=false → SL-MSG-04
routing_decision.last_minute_path: R037=true AND scheduledSendAt set → ScheduledLookupTrigger queued

slices.name: ExecuteSynchronousDispatch
business_rules_enforced: BR008 precondition — web path gate must be true before entry
StatusProgressionGuard active from first Message creation
isLookedUp=true must be set BEFORE gateway dispatch (SC001 guard)
outputs_effects: Messages created with resolved phone numbers and merge-applied text
slices.idempotency_concurrency: NOT idempotent. Guarded by SL-MSG-03 idempotency — Broadcast.active+isLookedUp prevent re-entry. isLookedUp=true written before dispatch to prevent SC001.
slices.domain_scope_note: PN-001: Email channel dispatch follows same orchestration but routes via EmailGateway adapter. BroadcastEmailContent is sms_derived_extension — not yet promoted to shared messaging candidate.

slices.name: EnqueueAsyncDispatch
business_rules_enforced: BR007 precondition — scheduledSendAt set OR batch path chosen
external_dependencies: Azure Service Bus / Azure Batch (infrastructure — enqueue message)
outputs_effects: BroadcastId enqueued to async lookup pipeline
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Safe to enqueue. Downstream SL-MSG-06 guards against duplicate processing via isLookedUp gate.

slices.name: ExecuteAsyncLookupAndDispatch
business_rules_enforced: isLookedUp gate — SHORT-CIRCUIT if already true (SC001 DualPipelineRaceCondition guard)
isLookedUp=true WRITTEN BEFORE gateway dispatch (atomicity requirement)
BR006 — large broadcast warning at >25000 Kvhx
StatusProgressionGuard from first Message creation
outputs_effects: Messages bulk-created with resolved phone numbers
slices.idempotency_concurrency: SC001: isLookedUp=true gate prevents double-dispatch if both sync and async pipelines race. CRITICAL ordering: set isLookedUp=true THEN dispatch to gateway. Bulk inserts use batch size from B018.

slices.name: ReceiveDeliveryStatusCallback
business_rules_enforced: R036 — MUST write via IDeliveryStatusWriter. Direct table write FORBIDDEN.
B014 — GatewayApi DLR: parse MessageId as LONG (not int). Int truncation bug documented — IDs >2.1B silently wrong.
DeliveryStatusEvent created with imported=false (pending progression evaluation)
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Gateways may retry callbacks. Idempotency guard: (messageId + gatewayReference + statusCode). Duplicate callbacks create duplicate DeliveryStatusEvents — StatusProgressionGuard handles ordering safely.
slices.ownership_note: PN-002: Messaging aggregate owns write-surface semantics. Email, Voice, Eboks channels call IDeliveryStatusWriter. They do NOT acquire shared ownership of DeliveryStatusEvent storage — contract access only.

slices.name: ProcessStatusProgression
business_rules_enforced: R035 + StatusProgressionGuard — statusCode may only increase in rank. Lower-rank callbacks rejected (imported=true, statusCode unchanged).
B016 — batch size 300 per run. Processes DeliveryStatusEvents where imported=false.
Terminal statuses (DELIVERED, FAILED_FINAL) block all further statusCode updates (immutable once terminal)
outputs_effects: Message.statusCode updated when new rank > current rank
slices.idempotency_concurrency: IDEMPOTENT per event (already-imported events are no-op). StatusProgressionGuard check must be atomic: read current rank + compare + update in single transaction to prevent concurrent race.
slices.system_concern: SC001 DualPipelineRaceCondition (StatusProgressionGuard is the primary defense)

slices.name: QueryBroadcastStatus
outputs_effects: Returns broadcast summary: recipientCount, sentCount, deliveredCount, failedCount
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: ManageCustomerHierarchy
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Creates not idempotent. Updates idempotent by entity ID.

slices.name: EvaluateProfileGate
business_rules_enforced: BR023 — BroadcastNoReciept: receipt email suppressed
BR024 — AlwaysOwner: forces sendToOwner=true
BR025 — DontSendEmail: forces email channel off
BR026 — CanSpecifyLookup: gates sendToOwner / sendToAddress fields
BR027 — CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate: gates private/business flags
outputs_effects: Returns effective permission set (EffectiveProfilePermissions) for ProfileId
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: ManageApiKeyAccess
business_rules_enforced: SECURITY — API key hash stored (OWASP A02). Cleartext NEVER persisted.
Cleartext returned exactly once at creation — caller must store immediately
outputs_effects: Cleartext key returned once at create — not retrievable after
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Create not idempotent. Revoke idempotent by key ID.
slices.security_note: OWASP A02: Rotate via new key + revoke old key. Key hash algorithm must be non-reversible (bcrypt / SHA-256 + salt).

slices.name: ManageStandardReceiver
business_rules_enforced: Keyword unique per country (SUB-E03 invariant)
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Updates idempotent by entity ID. Keyword create enforces uniqueness (conflict returns error, not duplicate).

slices.name: ManageReceiverPricing
slices.idempotency_concurrency: IDEMPOTENT — upsert by composite key.

slices.name: PrepareImportJob
business_rules_enforced: R042 — sizeKb < 10 → inline confirm path; sizeKb >= 10 → Azure Batch path (routing decision made at prepare time)
ImportJob status machine: Queued(1) → ProcessingColumns(2) → ColumnsReady(3) → ReadyForImport(4)
outputs_effects: Column mappings optionally pre-filled from ImportSavedConfiguration
slices.idempotency_concurrency: One job per file upload. Column detection idempotent given same file. Status machine prevents re-processing.

slices.name: ConfirmImportJob
business_rules_enforced: R041 — CommitStarted(107) written as crash-safe marker BEFORE any data write. Atomicity: marker write must succeed before data rows are committed.
R042 — sizeKb < 10 → run inline; sizeKb >= 10 → dispatch to Azure Batch (CreateConfirmDataImportAzureTaskAsync)
Only valid ImportRows committed to target aggregate
Terminal states DataImported(6) and Error(-1) reject re-run (guard against double-commit)
external_dependencies: Target aggregate for imported data (e.g. AGG-SUB for subscriber imports)
outputs_effects: Valid ImportRows committed to target aggregate
ImportJob.status = DataImported(6) on success OR Error(-1) on failure
slices.idempotency_concurrency: IDEMPOTENT via CommitStarted(107) resume: crash during data write → restart detects CommitStarted → routes to SL-IMP-03 ResumeImportJob. Double-commit prevented by terminal state check before entry.

slices.name: ResumeImportJob
business_rules_enforced: R041 — CommitStarted(107) is the resume entry condition. Only status=107 enters this path.
Resume reads last-committed row offset and continues from that position
outputs_effects: ImportJob completed from resume offset (equivalent end-state to SL-IMP-02)
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Designed for single concurrent execution. Multiple concurrent resumes are safe — CommitStarted terminal guard prevents double-commit.

slices.name: ExecuteAddressLookup
business_rules_enforced: R043 — only Kvhx string crosses adapter boundary. Country-native keys stay within adapter.
R023 — Kvhx string is canonical identity. Returns Kvhx, not DB surrogate.
SendToOwner on Broadcast determines whether AddressOwner data is fetched (via SL-ADDR-02)
outputs_effects: Returns list of (Kvhx, PhoneNumber?, OwnerName?) per RecipientCriterion
Unresolved criteria → reported as UnresolvedMessageRecord (MSG-E07) by caller
slices.idempotency_concurrency: IDEMPOTENT — reads canonical ResolvedAddress state. Same criteria yields same result unless address cache was updated by SL-ADDR-03.
slices.canonical_state_note: PN-003: This slice operates on canonical state only (ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress, ADDR-E02 AddressOwner). Country adapter staging tables (ADDR-E05 NorwegianAddress, ADDR-E06 SwedishAddress) are NOT accessible from this slice.

slices.name: LookupAddressOwner
business_rules_enforced: Only executed when Broadcast.lookupConfig.sendToOwner=true
BR024 — AlwaysOwner role forces sendToOwner=true before this slice executes
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.
slices.canonical_state_note: PN-003: Reads from ADDR-E02 AddressOwner (canonical cache, populated by SL-ADDR-03). Not from country adapter staging tables.

slices.name: RunCountryAddressImport
business_rules_enforced: R043 — adapter resolves country-native key to Kvhx before writing canonical state
AddressKvhxChange tracked when Kvhx renames detected
FI adapter (ADDR-E07) DEFERRED — no Finnish import until source replaced
outputs_effects: ADDR-E03 AddressKvhxChange records created for detected renames
Country adapter staging tables updated (adapter-internal — not exposed to caller)
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Idempotent per source batch. Kvhx renames detected and applied. Staging tables are adapter-internal.
slices.adapter_staging_note: PN-003: NorwegianAddress (ADDR-E05) and SwedishAddress (ADDR-E06) staging data is adapter-internal. The only output of this slice visible to Messaging is updated canonical ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress.

slices.name: RouteToCountryAdapter
business_rules_enforced: R043 — IAddressAdapter per country. Core dispatches by Broadcast.countryId.
FI — DEFERRED: route to NullAdapter / throw NotSupportedException until source replaced
outputs_effects: Returns Kvhx-based result set to SL-ADDR-01
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Routing is stateless. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: ManageProspectLifecycle
outputs_effects: Contact persons, products, user role pre-configs managed
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Creates not idempotent. Status transitions must respect ProspectStatus FSM.

slices.name: ConvertProspectToCustomer
business_rules_enforced: R038 — Two-step NON-ATOMIC: PATCH 1 = set Prospect.customerId + COPY ContactPersons to Customer. PATCH 2 = REPLACE CustomerProducts from ProspectProducts (destructive).
R039 — ProcessTasks NOT transferred at conversion. LEAVE ON PROSPECT. Customer starts clean. Implementing task migration requires explicit new business requirement.
R040 — ProspectUserRoleMappings NOT activated on Customer. Historical record only. Implementing role auto-activation requires explicit new business requirement.
Gap feature ConversionWithoutTaskMigration† — verified absence, not omission.
Gap feature ConversionWithoutRoleActivation† — verified absence, not omission.
external_dependencies: AGG-CUST (Customer + CustomerAccount — created or linked in PATCH 1)
outputs_effects: PATCH 1: Prospect.customerId set. ContactPersons COPIED (not moved) to Customer.
PATCH 2: CustomerProducts REPLACED from ProspectProducts (destructive — existing CustomerProducts deleted first).
ProcessTasks remain on Prospect — zero migration.
ProspectUserRoleMappings remain on Prospect — zero activation.
slices.idempotency_concurrency: NOT idempotent end-to-end — PATCH 2 is destructive. Guard against re-run: check Prospect.status before executing PATCH 2. PATCH 1 alone is idempotent by Prospect.customerId already set.

slices.name: ManageProcessTask
business_rules_enforced: R039 — ownerType must be in ProcessTaskTemplate.allowedOwnerTypes (application-layer. No DB FK.)
ownerId must reference a valid entity of the declared ownerType — verified at command time
outputs_effects: ProcessTask created / updated / completed under polymorphic ownerType+ownerId
slices.idempotency_concurrency: Create not idempotent. Complete is idempotent (already-completed task is no-op).

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: BroadcastActivation → AddressLookup
cross_aggregate_coordination.description: SL-MSG-03 ActivateBroadcast → SL-ADDR-01 ExecuteAddressLookup (via SL-ADDR-04 RouteToCountryAdapter). Messaging aggregate calls Address aggregate. Returns Kvhx strings only — not address structs.
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R043 — Kvhx string is the only data that crosses the AGG-MSG / AGG-ADDR boundary

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: DeliveryStatus → Messaging (cross-channel)
cross_aggregate_coordination.description: Email, Voice, Eboks channels write delivery status via IDeliveryStatusWriter. Called from infrastructure layer, not from another aggregate. Messaging retains ownership of the write-surface semantics.
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R036/CAC-001 — all channel status writes via contract. No shared table ownership.

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: BroadcastCompose → ProfileGate
cross_aggregate_coordination.description: SL-MSG-01 and SL-MSG-03 call SL-CUST-02 EvaluateProfileGate to determine effective permissions before creating or activating a broadcast. Read-only cross-aggregate query.
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: CAC-003 ProfileGate — checked at both compose and activate

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: ImportConfirm → Target Aggregate
cross_aggregate_coordination.description: SL-IMP-02 ConfirmImportJob commits validated ImportRows to the target aggregate (e.g. AGG-SUB for subscriber data). The import aggregate does not own the target data — it orchestrates the write.
cross_aggregate_coordination.contract: Typed domain command per import type
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: Import aggregate owns import lifecycle only — not target data

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: ProspectConversion → Customer
cross_aggregate_coordination.description: SL-PIPE-02 ConvertProspectToCustomer creates or links a Customer entity in AGG-CUST as part of PATCH 1. Two-step, non-atomic. Customer creation succeeded does not imply task/role migration — those are explicit non-events (R039, R040).
cross_aggregate_coordination.contract: Prospect.customerId set after Customer entity confirmed
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R038/CAC-005 — two-step non-atomic. No implicit cascade from Prospect to Customer for tasks or roles.
